title: Continuous velocity joint having disconnect capability
abstract: A continuous velocity joint assembly comprises an input shaft and an output shaft. A continuous velocity mechanism allows transfer of power between the input and output shafts while allowing angular variation between the shafts. A disconnect arrangement having first and second portions capable of relative rotation with respect to one another in a disconnected state and incapable of relative rotation with respect to one another in a connected state is also provided. An actuator causes the disconnect arrangement to transition between the disconnected state and the connected state, whereby power is transferred between the input and output shafts in the connected state and power is not transferred between the input and output shafts in the disconnected state. At least one bearing may interpose the first portion and the second portion.
claims:
Claims CLAIM What is claimed is: 1. A continuous velocity joint assembly comprising: an input shaft and an output shaft; a continuous velocity mechanism that allows transfer of power between the input and output shafts while allowing angular variation between the shafts; a disconnect arrangement having first and second portions capable of relative rotation with respect to one another in a disconnected state and incapable of relative rotation with respect to one another in a connected state; and an actuator causing the disconnect arrangement to transition between the disconnected state and the connected state, whereby the power is transferred between the input and output shafts in the connected state and power is not transferred between the input and output shafts in the disconnected state. 2. A continuous velocity joint assembly as set forth in claim 1, wherein the continuous velocity mechanism has an outer housing in which a shaft carrying multiple trunnions is received, the first and second portions being associated with the outer housing. 3. A continuous velocity joint assembly as set forth in claim 2, wherein at least part of the first portion and at least part of the second portion are concentric with one another. 4. A continuous velocity joint assembly as set forth in claim 3, wherein the first portion has an axial extension integral with the outer housing and the second portion is concentric with the axial extension. 5. A continuous velocity joint assembly as set forth in claim 2, wherein the first and second portions are axially aligned with one another. 6. A continuous velocity joint assembly as set forth in claim 1, wherein the disconnect assembly includes a clutch mechanism that causes selective coupling between the first and second portions. 7. A continuous velocity joint assembly as set forth in claim 1, wherein the clutch mechanism includes a slidable collar slidably moveable on the second portion between the disconnected state and the connected state. 8. A continuous velocity joint assembly as set forth in claim 6, wherein the clutch mechanism includes an axially-movable linkage engaging the slidable collar. 9. A continuous velocity joint assembly as set forth in claim 7, wherein the clutch mechanism includes a linear actuator causing axial movement of the linkage and thus the slidable collar. 10. A continuous velocity joint as set forth in claim 8, wherein the linear actuator comprises a screw-type actuator. 11. A continuous velocity joint as set forth in claim 8, wherein the linear actuator comprises a solenoid. 12. A continuous velocity joint as set forth in claim 8, wherein the solenoid comprises a bistable solenoid. 13. A continuous velocity assembly as set forth in claim 1, wherein at least one bearing interposes the first portion and the second portion. 14. A continuous velocity joint assembly comprising: an input shaft and an output shaft; a continuous velocity mechanism that allows transfer of power between the input and output shafts while allowing angular variation between the shafts, the continuous velocity mechanism having an outer housing in which a shaft carrying multiple trunnions is received; 

 a disconnect arrangement having first and second portions capable of relative rotation with respect to one another in a disconnected state and incapable or relative rotation with respect to one another in a connected state, the first portion is integral with the outer housing, the disconnect assembly including a clutch mechanism that causes selective coupling between the first and second portions; and an actuator causing the disconnect arrangement to transition between the disconnected state and the connected state, whereby the power is transferred between the input and output shafts in the connected state and power is not transferred between the input and output shafts in the disconnected state. 15. A continuous velocity joint assembly as set forth in claim 14, wherein at least part of the first portion and at least part of the second portion are concentric with one another. 16. A continuous velocity joint assembly as set forth in claim 15, wherein the first portion is formed as an axial extension integral with a bell portion of the outer housing and the second portion is concentric with the axial extension. 17. A continuous velocity joint assembly as set forth in claim 15, wherein the first and second portions are axially aligned with one another. 18. A continuous velocity joint assembly as set forth in claim 14, wherein the clutch mechanism includes a slidable collar' slidably moveable on the second portion between the disconnected state and the connected state. 19. A continuous velocity joint assembly as set forth in claim 18, wherein the clutch mechanism includes an axially-movable linkage engaging the slidable collar. 20. A continuous velocity joint assembly as set forth in claim 19, wherein the clutch mechanism includes a linear actuator causing axial movement of the slidable collar.
